metadata.description: Oracle Skills marketplace — installable, source-backed skills for Oracle technologies. Each top-level domain folder is published as its own plugin.

plugins.name: apex
plugins.description: Oracle APEX skills for Oracle APEX application development, including the APEXlang router with deterministic local-context discovery and compact machine-readable contracts.

plugins.name: db
plugins.description: Oracle Database guidance for SQL, PL/SQL, SQLcl, ORDS, administration, app development, performance, security, migrations, and agent-safe database workflows. Use when the user asks to write, edit, rewrite, review, format, debug, tune, or explain SQL; create or refactor PL/SQL; use SQLcl, Liquibase, ORDS, JDBC, node-oracledb, Python, Java, .NET, or database frameworks; troubleshoot queries, sessions, locks, waits, indexes, optimizer plans, AWR, ASH, migrations, schemas, users, roles, privileges, backup, recovery, Data Guard, RAC, multitenant, containers, monitoring, auditing, encryption, VPD, or safe agent database operations.

plugins.name: oracle-dev
plugins.description: Project-specific Oracle Database schema and SQL development standards. Primary entry point for application schema work — project naming, keys, audit columns, and idempotent DDL take precedence over generic db/ guidance. Pairs with and depends on the db/ plugin for administration, performance, PL/SQL, ORDS, SQLcl, and other topics outside project schema standards.

plugins.name: fusion
plugins.description: Oracle Fusion domain. Placeholder for future Oracle Fusion skills.

plugins.name: graal
plugins.description: Build, configure, and troubleshoot GraalVM Native Image applications. Use this skill for native-image CLI builds, Maven or Gradle Native Build Tools, reachability metadata, and build or run time issue diagnosis.

plugins.name: oci
plugins.description: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure guidance for OKE, OCI IoT Platform digital twins and publish flows, Enterprise AI models, agents, RAG, governance, and Oracle platform integrations.
